question: What is the hospital name and where is it located?
answer: City General Hospital is located at 42 MG Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560001. We are near the MG Road Metro Station, making us easily accessible by public transport.

question: What is the hospital phone number?
answer: Our main reception can be reached at +91-80-2345-6789. For emergencies, please call our emergency helpline at +91-80-2345-6700, which is available 24/7.

question: What are the visiting hours?
answer: General visiting hours are 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM daily. ICU visiting is restricted to two time slots: 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM and 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM. A maximum of two visitors per patient are allowed at a time.

question: What are the emergency room hours?
answer: Our Emergency Room is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year. For medical emergencies, please call +91-80-2345-6700 or visit us directly.

question: What departments and specialties are available?
answer: We have the following departments: Cardiology, Dermatology, Pediatrics, Neurology, Orthopedics, General Medicine, Gynecology, ENT (Ear Nose Throat), Ophthalmology, Psychiatry, Oncology, Urology, and Gastroenterology. Each department is staffed with experienced specialists.

question: Is parking available at the hospital?
answer: Yes, we have a multi-level parking facility with 500+ spaces. Parking is available for both two-wheelers and four-wheelers. The first 2 hours are free for patients and visitors. After that, parking is charged at Rs. 30 per hour. Valet parking is also available at the main entrance for Rs. 100.

question: How do I get to the hospital by public transport?
answer: City General Hospital is a 5-minute walk from MG Road Metro Station (Purple Line). Bus routes 335, 500C, and 201 stop directly in front of the hospital. Auto-rickshaws and ride-sharing services can drop you at our main gate on MG Road.

question: What insurance plans do you accept?
answer: We accept most major insurance providers including Star Health, ICICI Lombard, HDFC Ergo, New India Assurance, Bajaj Allianz, Max Bupa, and Religare Health. We also accept government schemes like Ayushman Bharat and CGHS. Please bring your insurance card and a valid ID when visiting. Our billing desk can verify your coverage on the spot.

question: What payment methods are accepted?
answer: We accept cash, all major credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, RuPay), UPI payments (Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm), net banking, and demand drafts. EMI options are available for bills above Rs. 50,000 through select bank credit cards.

question: Is there a pharmacy in the hospital?
answer: Yes, we have an in-house pharmacy located on the ground floor near the main lobby. It is open from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily. A 24-hour emergency pharmacy is available near the Emergency Room for urgent medication needs.

question: Is there a cafeteria or canteen?
answer: Yes, our cafeteria is located on the 2nd floor and is open from 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM. We serve vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals, snacks, and beverages. Special dietary meals can be arranged for admitted patients through the nursing station.

question: How do I access my medical records?
answer: You can access your medical records through our patient portal at portal.citygeneralhospital.in. You will need your patient ID and registered phone number to log in. Alternatively, you can request physical copies at the Medical Records department on the 1st floor, which takes 2-3 working days. A nominal fee of Rs. 100 applies for physical copies.

question: How do I access the patient portal?
answer: Visit portal.citygeneralhospital.in and click 'Register'. You will need your patient ID (found on your hospital card or discharge summary) and your registered mobile number. An OTP will be sent for verification. Once registered, you can view appointments, lab reports, prescriptions, and billing history.

question: What is the visitor policy?
answer: Each patient is allowed a maximum of 2 visitors at a time during visiting hours (8 AM - 8 PM). Visitors must collect a visitor pass from the reception desk. Children under 12 are not permitted in the ICU. All visitors must follow hygiene protocols including hand sanitization.

question: Do you have ambulance services?
answer: Yes, we operate a fleet of 10 ambulances including 4 Advanced Life Support (ALS) units. For ambulance services, call +91-80-2345-6700. Our ambulances cover all areas within a 30 km radius of the hospital. The average response time is 15-20 minutes within Bengaluru city limits.

question: How do I get a referral or second opinion?
answer: You can request a referral from your treating doctor, who will recommend the appropriate specialist. For a second opinion, you can book a consultation with any of our specialists directly through our appointment line at +91-80-2345-6789. Please bring all previous medical records, test reports, and imaging for the consultation.

question: What are the room types and charges?
answer: We offer General Ward (Rs. 1,500/day), Semi-Private rooms (Rs. 3,500/day), Private rooms (Rs. 6,000/day), and Deluxe Suites (Rs. 10,000/day). All rooms include meals, nursing care, and Wi-Fi. Private rooms and above include a dedicated attendant bed. ICU charges are Rs. 8,000/day.

question: How do I get my lab reports or test results?
answer: Lab reports are typically available within 24-48 hours depending on the test. You can access them through our patient portal at portal.citygeneralhospital.in, or collect physical copies from the Lab Reports counter on the ground floor. Reports can also be sent to your registered email upon request.

question: What should I bring for a hospital admission?
answer: Please bring a valid government ID (Aadhaar, passport, or driving license), your insurance card if applicable, any previous medical records and prescriptions, a list of current medications, comfortable clothing, and personal toiletries. The hospital provides bedding, towels, and meals.

question: Do you offer health check-up packages?
answer: Yes, we offer several health check-up packages: Basic Health Check (Rs. 2,500), Comprehensive Health Check (Rs. 5,000), Executive Health Check (Rs. 8,500), and Women's Wellness Package (Rs. 6,000). Packages include consultations, blood tests, imaging, and a detailed health report. Appointments can be booked through our reception.

question: Is Wi-Fi available in the hospital?
answer: Yes, free Wi-Fi is available throughout the hospital for patients and visitors. Connect to the network 'CityGeneral-Guest' and register with your mobile number. The Wi-Fi is available 24/7.

question: What are the OPD (outpatient) timings?
answer: OPD consultations are available Monday to Saturday, 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM and 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. Some specialists also offer evening OPD from 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM. OPD is closed on Sundays and public holidays. Emergency services are available round the clock.

question: How do I file a complaint or give feedback?
answer: You can share feedback or file a complaint through our patient portal, by emailing feedback@citygeneralhospital.in, or by visiting the Patient Relations desk on the ground floor. You can also call our feedback helpline at +91-80-2345-6780. We aim to acknowledge all complaints within 24 hours and resolve them within 7 working days.

question: Do you have a blood bank?
answer: Yes, City General Hospital has a licensed blood bank that operates 24/7. We maintain stocks of all blood groups. If you wish to donate blood, walk-in donations are accepted from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM daily. Donors must be between 18-65 years old and weigh at least 50 kg.

question: What COVID-19 protocols are in place?
answer: All visitors must wear masks in clinical areas. Hand sanitizer stations are available at all entrances and floors. COVID-19 testing is available at our lab. Patients with COVID symptoms are directed to a separate screening area. Vaccination services are available at our Immunization Center on the 1st floor.

question: Do you offer teleconsultation or online appointments?
answer: Yes, we offer teleconsultation services through our patient portal or the CityGeneral app. Video consultations are available for follow-up visits and non-emergency consultations. Fees are the same as in-person OPD visits. Prescriptions are sent digitally after the consultation.

question: What is the discharge process?
answer: The discharge process typically takes 2-4 hours. Your treating doctor will initiate the discharge. The nursing team will provide discharge instructions and medications. Final billing is prepared by our billing department. You will receive a discharge summary with all treatment details, prescriptions, and follow-up instructions.

question: Is there accommodation for patient attendants?
answer: One attendant is allowed to stay with the patient in Semi-Private and Private rooms at no extra charge. In General Ward, attendants can use the waiting lounge on each floor. We also have a guest house nearby with rooms available at Rs. 800/night for outstation patient families. Contact reception for guest house bookings.
